commit.message:
Fix indirect eval scoping: suppress globalThis writes for direct eval inside functions

When direct eval runs inside a function, var declarations should stay in the
function's variable environment per §19.2.1.3 EvalDeclarationInstantiation.
Previously, DefineGlobal/DefineGlobalSoft/SetGlobal always mirrored non-lexical
bindings to globalThis, causing indirect eval to see the direct eval's local
vars instead of the true global scope.

Added eval_fn_scope flag to VM, set when creating eval VM for direct eval
now check this flag and skip globalThis writes when set.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
ci: rename workflow, add missing CHANGELOG entries (#20)

* ci: auto-update CHANGELOG on release

When the CI workflow runs via workflow_dispatch (manual release), the
release job now moves CHANGELOG.md Unreleased entries to a versioned
section (e.g. '## v1.2.0 (2026-04-08)') before creating the tag.
The changelog commit is pushed to main, and the tag is created on
the updated commit.

This ensures the tagged commit always has its version in the
changelog, without requiring a manual changelog update before
triggering the release.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: add missing CHANGELOG entries for PRs #16 and #18

Add entries for proxy auto-reconnect, consolidated CI pipeline,
Go 1.26, Node.js 24, pre-commit hook, and CI lint checks.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: rename workflow from CI to Build/Test/Release

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: decouple deprecated filter and preserve regions in scraper

Fix 1: Deprecated filtering now runs unconditionally via enrichment
config (model_enrichment.json) instead of being coupled to
--merge-regions. The deprecated list is the authoritative blocklist.
merge_region_data() now only handles region merging and logs any
deprecated models it discovers for manual addition to the config.

Fix 2: _preserve_existing_regions() loads prior output JSON before
writing and carries forward non-empty region data for models that
would otherwise lose it. This makes --merge-regions additive rather
than all-or-nothing.

Fix 3: model-catalog-sync.md workflow updated to use
--include-partners and write to both docs/ and docs-vnext/.

Tests: 12 new tests covering filter_deprecated() and
_preserve_existing_regions() (97 total pass).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: replace azuredevops_agent_queues with azuredevops_agent_queue data source

The plural data source does not exist in the azuredevops provider.
Switch to the singular azuredevops_agent_queue which looks up by name.

Since the singular data source errors if the queue doesn't exist,
introduce a boolean variable ado_queue_exists (default: false).
- First run / new project: ado_queue_exists=false → resource created normally
- Re-run with lost state: ado_queue_exists=true → data source used, resource skipped

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: replace broken JsonSerializerContext emission with JsonSerializerOptions

Source generators can't see each other's output, so emitting
partial class Xxx : JsonSerializerContext with [JsonSerializable]
attributes never worked in real projects — the STJ source gen
never processed them.

Changed all 3 emitters (JsonContext, ModuleJsonContext, ChainOfThought)
to emit static JsonSerializerOptions helpers instead. Fixed sample
to use source-generated GetPredictors()/CloneCore() instead of
hand-written reflection hacks. Sample now runs E2E with source gen.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix yield* return delegation to inner iterator

Three issues fixed:

1. The yield* SetupTry handler was marked as catch-only (flags=0), so
   handle_throw skipped it when generator_return_pending was set. Changed
   to for_finally=true so the delegation handler is always entered.

2. Added ClearGeneratorReturn opcode emitted at the start of the return
   delegation path. This allows errors thrown during return delegation
   (e.g., inner .return() throwing, returning non-object, getter errors)
   to be caught by user catch handlers instead of being skipped.

3. In the 'return is undefined' path, restore generator_return_pending
   via SetGeneratorReturn before throwing so the return completion
   properly propagates through finally handlers.

Fixes all 10 yield* return delegation tests in cluster 15.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix initializer-safe bulk renaming and NameFixPass initializer iteration (#369)

## Summary

Fixes onnx/ir-py#353.

This PR addresses two related rename failures:

1. Direct rename loops fail for initializer-backed values in
swap/permutation cases.
2. `NameFixPass` can raise `RuntimeError: dictionary keys changed during
iteration` when a rename changes initializer dict keys while the pass is
iterating them.

## Changes

- add `onnx_ir.convenience.rename_values()` for bulk value renaming
- make initializer-backed multi-value renames safe by using temporary
names during swaps/permutations
- keep the existing initializer collision checks for names outside the
rename set
- make `NameFixPass` iterate over a snapshot of initializer values
instead of the live dict view
- add regression tests for:
  - swapping two initializer-backed outputs
- renaming to an existing initializer name before running `NameFixPass`

## Why

A single `value.name = ...` rename is not enough for safe initializer
swaps, because the initializer name guard correctly rejects taking
another live initializer name. The new bulk helper provides the missing
multi-rename operation, while the `NameFixPass` change makes renaming
safe when initializer dict keys are updated during the pass.

## Validation

- `pytest -q src/onnx_ir/_convenience/_convenience_test.py -k
rename_values`
- `pytest -q src/onnx_ir/passes/common/naming_test.py -k
initializer_collision`

Reported from downstream integration work in jax2onnx.

---------

Signed-off-by: enpasos <matthias.unverzagt@enpasos.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Justin Chu <justinchuby@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Implement comprehensive authentication system with Google Sign-In and onboarding

Features implemented:
- AuthViewModel with state management (AuthState sealed class)
- AuthenticationRepository centralizing Firebase operations
- Email/password auth with robust validation
- Google Sign-In integration with OAuth credentials
- OnboardingFragment for post-registration profile collection
- Password recovery functionality
- Session persistence with DataStore
- Comprehensive error handling with Italian user messages
- Material Design 3 UI with proper loading states
- Navigation graph updated with onboarding flow
- DI pattern using AuthViewModelProvider interface

Technical details:
- Uses Kotlin Coroutines and StateFlow for async operations
- Dual persistence: DataStore for preferences, Room for durable data
- ActivityResultContract for safe Google Sign-In
- Password strength validation (8+ chars, uppercase, number)
- Onboarding data validation (name, weight 30-300kg, height 100-250cm)
- All Firebase exceptions mapped to user-friendly messages

Files created:
- AuthViewModel.kt
- AuthenticationRepository.kt
- OnboardingFragment.kt
- fragment_onboarding.xml
- ic_google.xml
- edit_text_background.xml
- AUTHENTICATION_IMPLEMENTATION.md (comprehensive docs)

Files modified:
- AuthFragment.kt (complete rewrite with Google SignIn)
- MainActivity.kt (DI implementation)
- fragment_auth.xml (enhanced layout)
- nav_graph.xml (onboarding actions)
- build.gradle.kts (added Google Play Services Auth)
- strings.xml (40+ error/UI strings)

Note: Build requires Java 11, 17, or 21 (Java 26 has Kotlin compiler compatibility issues)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix FCM token registration — use Tasks.await() instead of addOnSuccessListener

addOnSuccessListener requires a Looper-attached thread but login runs on
Dispatchers.IO (no Looper), so the callback silently never fired.
Tasks.await() works correctly on IO threads. Also added detailed logs
under tag 'FCM' so registration flow is visible in Logcat.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(analysis): add pipeline chain inlay hints

Track which Call nodes in HIR were desugared from the |> pipeline
operator by recording their HirIds in a HashSet during lowering.
Thread the set through LowerResultMeta → TypedHir so that the inlay
hints module can identify them without changing the HIR structure.

For multi-line pipelines, emit a ChainedPipeline inlay hint at the end
of each LHS expression showing the intermediate type flowing into the
next step. Single-line pipelines are suppressed by comparing line
numbers in the existing span information.

- Add pipeline_call_ids: HashSet<HirId> to LowerResultMeta and
  LoweringContext; populate in lower_pipeline_operator
- Introduce HintCtx struct to bundle type_table, range, and
  pipeline_call_ids through hint collection helpers
- Add InlayHintKind::ChainedPipeline; wire up in LSP server and
  WASM bindings ("chainedPipeline" string kind)
- Add tests for multi-line and single-line pipeline hint behaviour

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: switch azuredevops provider auth from PAT to Crossplane SP

The PAT no longer needs Service Connections scope.
PAT is still stored in Key Vault and used at runtime by ArgoCD and
the AZP_TOKEN agent pod registration — those cannot use an SP.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(memory): inject L1+L2 project memory into AI assistant (mem-7)

- AiAssistantPanel: before sending custom question to ai-project-assistant,
  calls memory-context endpoint to fetch L1 (ai_context_summary) and
  L2 (last 10 memory entries) for the project
- Injects memory_summary and memory_recent into AI context payload
- ai-project-assistant: updated instructions to explicitly use memory
  context (decisions, preferences, events from project history)
- Graceful fallback: memory fetch failure is non-fatal, AI still answers

F1 Sprint status:
  mem-1 done (migration 113 - project_memory_entries)
  mem-2 done (migration 114 - voice_note_to_memory triggers)
  mem-3 done (netlify/functions/memory-context.ts)
  mem-4 done (netlify/functions/memory-add.ts)
  mem-5 done (netlify/functions/memory-check.ts)
  mem-6 done (src/features/projects/components/ProjectMemoryPanel.tsx)
  mem-7 DONE (this commit - L1+L2 injected into AI assistant calls)
  mem-8 done (ProjectDetail memory tab already implemented)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
